name: Risotto, Saffron
recipeInstructions:
1. Heat the stock in a medium pot and keep it barely simmering.
2. Mix half a cup of hot stock with the saffron; stir and set aside.
3. In a large skillet or soup pot, melt 3 tablespoons butter over medium heat. Add shallots and salt. Cook until shallots are translucent and soft (do not brown).
4. Add rice to the skillet and stir until well coated and slightly translucent, about 1-2 minutes.
5. Add a ladleful of stock to the rice and cook, stirring constantly, until most of the stock is absorbed. Continue this process, always stirring and maintaining a gentle simmer, until the rice is al dente (tender but with a firm core). Taste after approximately 4 cups of stock have been absorbed.
6. Stir in the remaining butter and Parmesan cheese. Serve immediately.
We recommend serving this with roasted vegetables, such as sliced red peppers and quartered small red onions. A simple green salad and good crusty French bread complement the meal well.
